incident_localization_order: Start with the watchdog CI gate.
Read the failing watchdog surface name before individual snapshots.
Classify the break as omission, drift, or partial_visibility.
For terminal or failed paths, prefer snapshot-backed terminal detail and normalized failure detail over sink snippets.
For manual stop-all incidents, confirm peer-plan propagation before treating one plan as isolated.
pre_shift_checks: Confirm fixed pins, promoted baseline, comparison source of truth, halt mode, and status delivery from the current status, report, and summary.
Confirm omission, drift, and partial_visibility remain the fixed operator meanings.
Confirm the active surface set remains the same nine watchdog surfaces.
Confirm the first deep proof remains the watchdog CI gate.
Confirm containment-first priority before sink-quality review.
Confirm the source-faithful seam remains Dexter paper_live and frozen Mew-X sim_live.
during_shift_checks: Start from the current failing watchdog surface name before opening snapshots.
Check containment surfaces before identity or sink quality.
Check handle_id and native_session_id continuity across handle, runtime, sink, terminal, and failure surfaces.
Check the fixed planned-runtime anchor fields against the planned envelope.
Check monotonic ack counts, sticky flags, and duplicate-or-terminal visibility.
Check sink fan-in only after containment and identity remain coherent.

classification_rules.omission: A required field or required surface is missing.
classification_rules.drift: A required field is present but no longer matches the planned source-faithful envelope.
classification_rules.partial_visibility: The lifecycle happened, but the operator-facing surface did not preserve all required detail.

halt: normalized failure detail loses identity continuity
shift-start fixed inputs disagree across status, report, or proof
quarantine: sink-only omission is reclassified as partial_visibility
ack-history detail becomes incomplete while containment remains intact
sink fan-in loses required detail while runtime and terminal still retain it
peer-plan stop propagation is not yet visible
continue: status, report, summary, contract, and proof stay aligned
no unresolved omission or drift remains
contained-only continuation is recorded explicitly when the incident is still open

reasons: The checklist now fixes what the operator should read and do during one shift, but the repository still lacks one bounded outgoing and incoming handoff record.
A handoff template is the smallest next cut that keeps the same proof trail, decision fields, and entry-point order consistent across shift changes.
It can be written without reopening runtime behavior, evidence collection, or source ownership.
An acceptance pack would mostly restate the same bounded surfaces before the new shift-close record is standardized.
The acceptance pack can become sharper after the handoff template fixes the minimum outgoing and incoming operator record.
The executor boundary is already bounded by the earlier transport spec and the watchdog CI gate.
The remaining gap is operator continuity, not code-boundary ambiguity.
